reviews.review_msg: Profs a decent guy, but used some confusing terms during lecture. Ws advertised as an intro to university, but felt like the opposite, a joke. Felt more like a 3rd year course. Textbook & readings were pointless. We did 2 in-class essays + journal posts & a mock exam. Attendance also matters. Save your time & money & don't take this.

Fall 2025 was the first time this course ever ran, so it's a pilot program. It's a Pass/Fail class. Kept to the syllabus. He was pretty passionate. Gave feedback on assignments/tests. I feel like he helped improve my writing because I actually listened to what he had to say LOL. Worth the money IMO.

No! just a big no, I am not going to say what I got in his class but to be honest do not take FASS100 it was a huge waste of time and caused more stress than my bigger classes such as psych. BUT DO YOURSELF A FAVOUR AND DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. He did not follow the syllabus and was pulling different things related and non related to the course :/

Funny guy but why did he make us read so many random papers and not teach us anything that was promised in the advertisement of this course? Save your money and just take another writing class (this didn't even count for a writing credit despite requiring multiple in-class essays). Also requires you to use pen and paper (no devices).

Would not recommend taking this class. Huge waste of time. The profs ok I guess. Readings for the most useless topics. The whole class was advertised as something it was not. We learned about citations and he just kept on saying how he could not really teach this. Also the survival kit was useless. Save your money!

This was supposed to be a class that eased your transition into higher education but it honestly made me want to run into the other direction. Would've been a good course for second year but for students coming into university it was very difficult to understand. I regret not dropping the course. Mark is a great professor but I wouldn't take this.
